UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a child user's profile information in the SkyboundKids Academy, allowing for personalized progress tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The display name of the child user.

avatarUrl.description: URL to the child's profile avatar or picture.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents a single educational lesson available in the SkyboundKids Academy, displayed as engaging cards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

description.description: A brief description or overview of the lesson.

contentHtml.description: The main content of the lesson, formatted as HTML to support rich text and media.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to an image thumbnail used for displaying the lesson card with cartoon-inspired design.

order.description: The sequential order of the lesson within a curriculum or series.

adminId.description: Reference to the administrator's ID (from an external authentication system) who uploaded this lesson. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N Lesson)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lesson was created/uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the lesson was last updated.

Quiz.title: Quiz
Quiz.description: Represents an interactive quiz for children, often associated with lessons, featuring multiple-choice questions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quiz entity.

description.description: A brief description or introduction to the quiz.

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson this quiz is primarily associated with. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N Quiz)

questions.description: A list of questions included in this quiz, each with options and a correct answer.

adminId.description: Reference to the administrator's ID (from an external authentication system) who uploaded this quiz. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N Quiz)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the quiz was created/uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the quiz was last updated.

QuizQuestion.title: QuizQuestion
QuizQuestion.description: Represents a single question within a Quiz, including its text, options, and the correct answer.

questionText.description: The text of the quiz question.

options.description: An array of possible answer options for the question.

correctAnswerIndex.description: The zero-based index of the correct answer within the 'options' array.

explanation.description: An optional explanation or feedback provided after the answer is revealed.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a practical or creative task for children to complete, supporting active learning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

description.description: Instructions or a brief overview of the task.

contentHtml.description: The detailed content and instructions for the task, formatted as HTML.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to an image thumbnail used for displaying the task card.

order.description: The sequential order of the task within a series or group.

adminId.description: Reference to the administrator's ID (from an external authentication system) who uploaded this task. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N Task)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the task was created/uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the task was last updated.

Lab.title: Lab
Lab.description: Represents an exploratory or experimental activity for children, encouraging hands-on learning and discovery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lab entity.

title.description: The title of the lab activity.

description.description: Instructions or a brief overview of the lab activity.

contentHtml.description: The detailed content and interactive elements for the lab, formatted as HTML.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to an image thumbnail used for displaying the lab card.

order.description: The sequential order of the lab within a series or group.

adminId.description: Reference to the administrator's ID (from an external authentication system) who uploaded this lab activity. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N Lab)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lab activity was created/uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the lab activity was last updated.

UserActivity.title: UserActivity
UserActivity.description: Tracks a child's interaction and progress with lessons, quizzes, tasks, and labs, including AI-powered encouragement messages.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserActivity entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the child performing the activity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserActivity)

activityType.description: The type of educational activity recorded (e.g., lesson completion, quiz attempt).

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson if the activity type is 'lesson_completed'. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N UserActivity)

quizId.description: Reference to the Quiz if the activity type is 'quiz_attempted'. (Relationship: Quiz 1:N UserActivity)

taskId.description: Reference to the Task if the activity type is 'task_completed'. (Relationship: Task 1:N UserActivity)

labId.description: Reference to the Lab if the activity type is 'lab_completed'. (Relationship: Lab 1:N UserActivity)

score.description: The score achieved in the activity, applicable for quizzes.

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the activity was completed or successfully passed.

aiMessage.description: The personalized AI-generated encouragement message stored after generation.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the user activity was recorded.

definition.description: Stores individual child user profiles. Access is restricted to the owner matching `request.auth.uid` via path-based ownership.

Globally accessible educational lessons. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access is restricted to administrators based on the `adminId` field and admin role. Includes denormalized 'adminId' for authorization.

Globally accessible interactive quizzes. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access is restricted to administrators based on the `adminId` field and admin role. Includes denormalized 'adminId' for authorization.

Individual questions for a quiz. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access is restricted to the quiz's `adminId`. Documents include denormalized `quizId` and `adminId` (copied from parent quiz) for authorization independence.

params.name: quizId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Quiz.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the QuizQuestion.

definition.description: Globally accessible practical tasks. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access is restricted to administrators based on the `adminId` field and admin role. Includes denormalized 'adminId' for authorization.

Globally accessible experimental lab activities. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access is restricted to administrators based on the `adminId` field and admin role. Includes denormalized 'adminId' for authorization.

Tracks a specific child's educational activities and progress. Access is strictly private to the `userId` (owner) embedded in the path and document fields. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile owner.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserActivity record.

definition.description: Designates users with administrator privileges. The existence of a document at `adminId` matching `request.auth.uid` grants admin status (DBAC pattern).

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds an administrator role.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for SkyboundKids Academy prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to core design principles. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design ensures authorization independence primarily through two mechanisms: path-based ownership for user-specific data and denormalized fields for shared or nested data management.

1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For `UserProfile` and `UserActivity` data, the `userId` is embedded directly in the collection path (`/userProfiles/{userId}`). This means that security rules can instantly verify `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing any `get()` calls to other documents. The `UserActivity` documents also contain a `userId` field, explicitly linking them to the owner, further simplifying rules to `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` or `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path).

2.  **Denormalized Admin Context for Nested Data:** For `QuizQuestion` documents, which are subcollections of `Quiz`, access control depends on the `adminId` of the parent `Quiz`. To achieve authorization independence as mandated, each `QuizQuestion` document will denormalize and store its parent's `adminId` (e.g., `quizAdminId`) and `quizId` directly within its own fields. This allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.quizAdminId` for write operations on questions without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `Quiz` document. This is critical for supporting atomic operations and avoiding complex, error-prone rule dependencies.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

The structure facilitates secure `list` operations (QAPs) by implementing **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture)**. 

1.  **Public Collections:** `lessons`, `quizzes`, `tasks`, and `labs` are structured as top-level collections (e.g., `/lessons/{lessonId}`). All documents within these collections share the same security posture: they are globally readable by all authenticated users (children) and only writable by designated administrators. This homogeneity allows for simple, performant `list` queries without requiring rules to filter results, as the entire collection adheres to a single read permission. Any authenticated child can read all lessons, quizzes, etc.

2.  **Private User Collections:** `userProfiles/{userId}` and `userProfiles/{userId}/activities/{activityId}` are strictly private. All documents in these collections are owned solely by the `userId` in the path. This segregation ensures that `list` operations on these collections automatically return only the data relevant to the authenticated user, as rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` for all reads and writes.

3.  **DBAC (Database Access Control for Admin Roles):** Admin roles are managed via a dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{adminId}`. The *existence* of a document at `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}` grants admin privileges. This is an explicit and easily debuggable way to manage global roles, allowing rules to check for `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` to grant elevated permissions to specific top-level collections (lessons, quizzes, tasks, labs) for write operations.
